ss01.description: A double storey g adds personality, but makes the overall feel of the face slightly less consistent.
ss01.label: Double Storey g

ss02.description: A single storey a gives the feel of the face more consistent, but takes away some of the horizontal focus and overall visual interest.
ss02.label: Single Storey Aa

ss03.description: The lowercaps e brings in more of a fun feeling, but adds complexity and negative space to a letter that usually provides structure.
ss03.label: Lowercaps E

ss04.description: The crossed t adds a visual reference to the original logo, but takes away some of the consistency in the letter group jft.
ss04.label: Crossed t

ss05.description: Straight terminals bring more harmony to the typeface, but remove the 13 degree anchor that's present throughout the typeface.
ss05.label: Straight terminals

ss06.description: A spurless “G” is a little more dynamic, but less structurally sound than its spurred brother. This is mostly a matter of preference.
ss06.label: Spurless G

ss07.description: A straight leg for the uppercase R gives a more geometric feel, but is a little less consistent with the uppercase A.
ss07.label: Straight Leg R

ss08.description: A 13 degree angle within the ABPR crossbars instead of a horizontal one. This is more unique, but gives a more quirky feel.
ss08.label: Diagonal ABPR

ss09.description: The lowercaps Y gets another lowercase glyph in the uppercase set. Because of the removal of its descender, it may take away some readability.
ss09.label: Lowercaps Y

ss10.description: A 13 degree angle within the punctuation makes a big impact at the end of every sentence. It closes, but not definitively so. This also impacts tittles on the i & j.
ss10.label: Angled Punctuation

ss11.description: Straight legged glyphs where angled legs are expected give the typeface a more vertical, geometric and stylised appearance.
ss11.label: Straight Legs KXY

ss12.description: Added crossbars in the upper and lowercase m’s are a more than subtle reference to the logo.
ss12.label: Logo Mm

ss13.description: These exaggerated crossbars bring a more dynamic appearance, but make the overall feeling a little less stable, a little less consistent.
ss13.label: Low Crossbars

ss14.description: Bowing terminals makes some letterforms curl back up, bringing a more playful touch. This does mean more whitespace and more difficult kerns.
ss14.label: Bowing Terminals
